channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Karoooos Cartrack Reports Record Subscriber Growth and Profit Boost in FY2027
item.description: Karoooos, a Singapore-based group owning Cartrack and Karoooos Logistics, reported record subscriber growth for its vehicle tracking business in FY2027. Cartrack's subscriber base grew 18% to over 2.8...

item.title: Tanzania-France partnership strengthens through culture, language, and investment
item.description: Tanzania and France are deepening their bilateral relationship through cultural exchange, language promotion, tourism growth, and strategic investments. The partnership evolved from traditional diplom...

item.title: Caitlin Clark Condemns Referees for Missed Call Leading to Contusion
item.description: Caitlin Clark, star player of the Indiana Fever, publicly criticized referees for failing to call a foul during a game against the Golden State Valkyries, which she claims resulted in a contusion. The...

item.title: Canadian Government Shifts to Support Oil Pipeline Amid Global Trade Pressures
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney's government has pivoted from hesitating to support a new oil pipeline, driven by geopolitical tensions and Alberta's economic concerns. The $40 billion project, led by fede...

item.title: NWHSA, Tana River Govt, and Stella Mundi Launch Climate-Smart Project for Jobs and Food Security
item.description: The National Water Harvesting and Storage Authority (NWHSA), Tana River County Government, and Stella Mundi Properties Ltd have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to implement the Lucerne Proj...

item.title: Consequences of Ghosting: Real Stories of Being Called Out and the Emotional Impact
item.description: The article compiles personal anecdotes from individuals who were ghosted by others, detailing how they were confronted by the people they had abandoned. These stories reveal the emotional turmoil and...

item.title: Nigeria's $500m Agriculture Fund Aims to Boost Food Security and Rural Development
item.description: Nigeria has launched a $500 million agriculture investment fund for the oil-producing Niger Delta region, aiming to increase food production, attract private investment, and enhance food security. Vic...

item.title: Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial: Witness Withdrawal Doesn't Guarantee Fewer Days
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte may not be shortened despite the prosecution's withdrawal of some witnesses, according to Senate impeachment court spokesperson Atty. Reginald Tong...

item.title: Why Strong Institutions Matter More Than Individual Leaders for Tanzania's Democratic Future
item.description: Makala hii ya maoni inaangalia namna Tanzania ilivyojenga urithi wa kisiasa uliotegemea maadili ya uwajibikaji, unyenyekevu na huduma kwa taifa tangu miaka ya mwanzo baada ya uhuru. Hata hivyo, mwandi...

item.title: Arrests over silver-laced soil theft from defunct Hindustan Photo Films factory in Nilgiris
item.description: Six men were arrested for stealing soil laced with silver from the defunct Hindustan Photo Films (HPF) factory in Nilgiris. The factory, operational since 1960, ceased operations in 2018 due to econom...

item.title: Ginger Rogers' Tennis Career: A Look at Her Competitions and Style
item.description: Ginger Rogers, renowned for her iconic Hollywood dance partnerships, was also a dedicated tennis player who competed at the highest level of American tennis. Her athletic prowess extended beyond the f...

item.title: Geelong Cats undermanned as St Kilda aims to end 13-game losing streak in AFL clash
item.description: In a highly anticipated AFL match at GMHBA Stadium, the Geelong Cats, missing key players Jeremy Cameron and Mark Blicavs, faced the St Kilda Saints in a crucial home game. The Cats, who have lost fiv...

item.title: JOOTRH Clarifies Blood Donation Process and Safety Measures
item.description: Jaramogi Oginga Odinga Teaching and Referral Hospital (JOOTRH) has explained the scientific process behind blood transfusions, addressing why donated blood cannot be used immediately. The hospital cla...

item.title: Wema Bank Advises Customers on Preventing Fake Apps and Phishing Scams

item.title: Trump welcomes release of detained American woman by Iran amid continuing regional tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump publicly thanked Iran after the release of Dena Karari, an American citizen who had been detained in Iran since December 2024. In a post on Truth Social, Trump described her ...

item.title: Democrats Outpace Republicans in Key Senate Races Despite Primaries Challenges
item.description: The article details Democrats' substantial fundraising advantages in pivotal Senate races across the U.S., with candidates like Jon Ossoff in Georgia, Roy Cooper in North Carolina, and James Talarico ...

item.title: Government proposes teacher salary adjustments to prevent industrial action
item.description: The article discusses the Victorian government's attempt to avert potential strikes among teachers by proposing a new pay offer. News Corp Australia's website encountered an issue where automated craw...

item.title: DCP Commends IEBC Conduct in Ol Kalou By-Election Amid Minor Procedural Concerns
item.description: The Democratic Congress Party (DCP) has praised the Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) for its smooth management of the ongoing Ol Kalou constituency by-election, despite a minor p...

item.title: Scientists Discover Sugar Molecule in Interstellar Space
item.description: Astronomers have detected a sugar molecule, specifically glycolaldehyde, in the interstellar space of the star-forming region Sagittarius B2. This discovery, published in *The Astrophysical Journal*, ...

item.title: Challenges Facing Tanzania's Legal Profession Amid Mega Projects
item.description: The article highlights how Tanzania's government has shifted legal responsibilities from private advocates to state attorneys, impacting the private bar. Since 2018, ministries have used in-house lega...
